title: Neuropathy Phenotyping Protocols - Immunohistochemistry for Tissue Sections
author: Eva Feldman
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7rfhm3n
materials: 2º antibody labeled with red or green fluorochrome.

steps.title: General Concepts
steps.instruction: General Concepts: • Keep buffers consistent through fix, rinses, antibody dilutions and chromogen development. • Antibodies are sticky, always use plastic tips and tubes for storing, diluting and pipeting antibodies. • Check slides after each major step in the protocol to make sure sections are still stuck to the slide. There is no point in continuing if the sections have all been removed due to over zealous washes.

steps.title: Cryosections
steps.instruction: Cryosections: 1. Remove slides from freezer, air dry. (warm plate) 2. Ring sections with PAP pen, if tissue was not fixed prior to embedding and sectioning, fix sections for 10-30 min, 4ºC ♦ 4% paraformaldehyde ♦ 2% paraformaldehyde ♦ 1:1 acetone: methanol, ice cold

steps.title: Hydrate through ethanol 100, 100, 95,...
steps.instruction: Hydrate through ethanol 100, 100, 95, 95, 70, 50, distilled water.

steps.title: Ring sections with PAP pen, carefully,...
steps.instruction: Ring sections with PAP pen, carefully, do not let sections dry.

steps.title: Rinse in 0
steps.instruction: Rinse in 0.5% H ² O ² in buffer (5 ml 30% in 300 ml buffer), 10 min (for immunofluorescence skip and go to blocking)

steps.title: Rinse 3 X 10 min in buffer
steps.instruction: Rinse 3 X 10 min in buffer.

steps.title: Permeabilize and block 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Permeabilize and block 10 minutes: ♦ 0.1% TX100 in buffer ♦ 2% non-fat dry milk ♦ 1% serum (serum from the animal in which the secondary antibody is raised e.g. 1º is rabbit, 2º goat anti-rabbit, block with normal goat)

steps.title: Diluted primary antibody in buffer containing 0
steps.instruction: Diluted primary antibody in buffer containing 0.1% TX100

steps.title: Remove slides from blocking solution, do...
steps.instruction: Remove slides from blocking solution, do not rinse, dab away excess fluid with filter paper, place 1º (20-40 ul) in PAP pen ring. Dilutions are antibody specific, check antibody book for suggested dilutions.

steps.title: Always wrap parafilm around the edge...
steps.instruction: Always wrap parafilm around the edge of the incubation chamber to seal: ♦ Incubation times: 1º 's overnight 22ºC, parafilm, 36-48 hours 4ºC, parafilm 2-4 hours 37ºC, lean toward 50-60 µl

steps.title: Rinse in buffer 3 X 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Rinse in buffer 3 X 10 minutes.

steps.title: Turn off overhead fluorescent lights to decrease quenching
steps.instruction: Turn off overhead fluorescent lights to decrease quenching.

steps.title: Incubate 1 hour 22ºC
steps.instruction: Incubate 1 hour 22ºC.

steps.title: Rinse 3 X 10 min buffer
steps.instruction: Rinse 3 X 10 min buffer.

steps.title: Rinse in buffer
steps.instruction: Rinse in buffer.

steps.title: Incubate 1 hour 22ºC
steps.instruction: Incubate 1 hour 22ºC.

steps.title: Rinse 3 X 10 min buffer
steps.instruction: Rinse 3 X 10 min buffer.

steps.title: Incubate in Avidin HRP I hour...
steps.instruction: Incubate in Avidin HRP I hour 22ºC **Prepare this solution 1 hour prior to use, it needs to equilibrate**

steps.title: Rinse 3 X 10 min buffer
steps.instruction: Rinse 3 X 10 min buffer.

steps.title: Rinse 3 X 10 min in...
steps.instruction: Rinse 3 X 10 min in buffer, collect first rinse and add bleach to denature DAB (DO NOT PUT BLEACH ON THE SLIDES)

steps.title: Dehydrate through graded ethanol 50, 70,...
steps.instruction: Dehydrate through graded ethanol 50, 70, 95, 95, 100, 100% and 2 changes of Hemo-D Counterstain.

steps.title: Mount coverslips to slides with Permount
steps.instruction: Mount coverslips to slides with Permount.
